results.claim: Champion-2 claimed 9.1% E2E at BS=32 in Round 1 argument based on inflated 838 GB/s measurement
OP-001 showed extreme Amdahl violation with 0.01x measured-vs-predicted ratio due to L2-warm micro-benchmarks
GEMV/GEMM projections dominate 78-94% of GPU time at all batch sizes
All batch sizes are deeply memory-bound with AI far below 419 FLOPs/byte crossover
Each decode step reads 7.6 GB of model weights through 152 linear projection GEMMs
All three champions independently converged on 676-693 GB/s Triton BW, resolving the 838 GB/s artifact
Delegate-2a identified L2 re-warming as root cause of inflated BW numbers before Round 1 critique
Champion-2 self-corrected in rebuttal: BS=32 speedup revised from 1.383x to 1.141x with 608 GB/s matching ncu ground truth
Champion-1 conceded and corrected L2 size from 48 MB to 96 MB after Champion-3 identified error
OP-002 post-gating E2E: BS=32 +5.51%, BS=8 +1.86%, BS=1 -0.57% (memory overhead, not kernel)
Validator independent kernel benchmark shows 1.097x at BS=8 and 1.197x at BS=32 with 400 MB L2 flush
11/11 correctness tests passed including CUDA graph capture/replay and adversarial inputs
SiLU fusion eliminates 75.5 MB intermediate tensor per layer at BS=32, saving 2.4 GB per decode step
State.json records OP-002 as GATED_PASS with 1.0551x E2E speedup and M>=8 dispatch
OP-001 E2E showed near-zero improvement: BS=1 +0.057%, BS=8 -0.052%, BS=32 +0.066%
Root cause: debate micro-experiments measured L2-warm standalone torch.mm, not production DRAM-bound F.linear
State records OP-001 failure: torch.mm vs F.linear dispatch gap
Champion-2 proposal claims 838 GB/s and 1.383x at BS=32 cold-cache
Champion-2 corrected in rebuttal: actual BS=32 speedup is 1.141x at 689 GB/s
Baseline cold BW reported at 1029 GB/s exceeding L40S DRAM peak
Champion-1 states L40S L2 is 48 MB, working set / L2 = 158x
Champion-1 conceded L40S has 96 MB L2 in Round 1 rebuttal
First baseline run produced sub-second latencies indicating configuration error
OP-001 required two commits, second being a critical torch.compile fix
Validator kernel benchmark went through multiple iterations
Delegate identified L2 re-warming as root cause of inflated BW numbers
Delegate mapped the dispatch interception point at utils.py:298-304
Delegate confirmed ba_proj is only 327 KB, not significant optimization target
Delegate-3a incorrectly states L40S L2 is 48 MB
Delegate-2a investigated BW discrepancy between champion-2 and champion-3
Delegate-3a independently investigated the same BW discrepancy
Final e2e_latency baselines have returncode=1 (failed to run)
Stage 1 baseline for BS=32 is 10.224s, not 10.270s from constraints.md
Stage 1 baseline for BS=1 is 6.494s, not 6.548s from constraints.md
Isolated benchmarks measured cuBLAS at 493-636 GB/s; production achieves 680-750 GB/s due to L2 pre-warming
torch.mm vs F.linear dispatch difference caused 10x overestimation of optimization opportunity for OP-001
All three candidates initially assumed L40S L2 = 48 MB; empirical measurement revealed 96 MB
Pre-transposing weights added 2.81 GB causing 0.57% BS=1 regression despite fused kernel never executing at BS=1
Campaign ran from 21:50 to 00:15 UTC across 7 stages
Qwen3.5-4B is a hybrid transformer with 24 DeltaRule and 8 full attention layers, dense SiLU-gated MLP
